summary: Iteration 26: Admin Portal Features & Subscription Auto-Create - ALL FEATURES WORKING. Tested: 1) GET /api/subscriptions/{guardian_id} auto-creates free plan with 10 seats if missing, 2) GET /api/admin/plan-stats returns plan breakdown with user counts, 3) GET /api/admin/users?search= filters users by name/email, 4) PUT /api/admin/users/{user_id}/subscription edits subscription, 5) PUT /api/admin/users/{user_id}/wallet sets wallet balance. Fixed critical JS error in AdminPortal.jsx where userSearch was used before initialization.
critical_code_review_comments: Fixed: userSearch state variable was used before initialization in AdminPortal.jsx - caused app crash on admin portal load
Plan Membership Overview shows: Total Parents, With Plans, No Plan, Total Students, and breakdown by plan type
User search filters users in real-time by name or email
Sub button opens Edit Subscription dialog with plan_name, student_seats, status fields
Wallet button opens Edit Wallet dialog with current balance and new balance input
Guardian Portal shows subscription with correct seats (2/25 for test user)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All admin portal features working. Subscription auto-create working. Fixed JS initialization error in AdminPortal. Plan stats show 57 total parents, 8 with plans, 49 without plans. Search filters work correctly. Edit subscription and wallet dialogs work correctly.

bug_fix_applied.issue: AdminPortal.jsx crashed with 'Cannot access userSearch before initialization' error
bug_fix_applied.root_cause: userSearch state variable was used in useQuery at line 44 but declared at line 116
bug_fix_applied.fix: Moved userSearch state declaration to line 25 (before useQuery hooks)
bug_fix_applied.verification: Admin portal now loads correctly without JS errors

mocked_apis.note: All APIs are real implementations, no mocking
